full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic Roll Protection System (RPS) enclosure-frame for a two-rider human-powered vehicle (tandem HPV). The RPS is a tubular roll cage that protects two riders during rollover and crash. The RPS must be a closed frame fully surrounding the occupant zone, constructed from 25.4 mm (1 in) OD x 2.41 mm (0.095 in) wall 4130 chromoly steel tubing, TIG-welded at nodes. Material: 4130 normalized steel, E = 205 GPa, yield = 460 MPa, ultimate = 670 MPa, density = 7850 kg/m^3. Geometry constraints: overall roll-hoop height above seat pan 1050 mm; tandem-rider cockpit length 2200 mm; helmet clearance 50 mm minimum to any hoop surface; vehicle braking capability independent of RPS (must decelerate from 25 km/h to rest in <= 6 m, included for completeness). Load cases: (R1) Top load: a 2670 N (600 lbf) load applied at 12 degrees from vertical at the top of the main roll hoop, aimed toward the driver. Maximum deflection at load-application point shall not exceed 5.1 cm (2 in, 51 mm). Max von Mises <= yield / 1.15 = 400 MPa. (R2) Side load: a 1330 N (300 lbf) load applied horizontally at the shoulder level of the main roll hoop. Max deflection at load point <= 3.8 cm (1.5 in, 38 mm). Max von Mises <= yield / 1.15 = 400 MPa. (R3) Harness-mount load: 1334 N (300 lbf) pulled rearward at the shoulder harness-attachment tab (simulating forward crash deceleration of the rider pulling on the harness). Max von Mises <= yield / 1.15 = 400 MPa. (R4) Combined top + side: 2670 N top + 1330 N side applied simultaneously; deflections must each meet their individual limits (<= 51 mm top, <= 38 mm side). Max von Mises <= ultimate / 1.5 = 447 MPa. (R5) RPS mass <= 7.5 kg. Primary verification: structural FEA, linear static, four independent + one combined load case. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 welded frame assembly treated as a single monolithic part, weld inspection certificate, and a report listing mass in kg, max deflection per load case in mm, max von Mises per load case in MPa.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic welded tubular Roll Protection System (RPS) for a two-rider human-powered vehicle (tandem HPV). The RPS is a closed frame around the tandem cockpit, built from 25.4 mm OD x 2.41 mm wall 4130 normalized chromoly steel, TIG-welded at nodes; four-point chassis mount.

Envelope: roll-hoop top >= 1050 mm above seat pan, tandem cockpit length 2200 mm, >= 50 mm helmet clearance to any hoop surface.

Load cases.
- LC1 top load: 2670 N (600 lbf) at 12 deg from vertical at the top of the main hoop, aimed at the driver; load-point deflection <= 51 mm (2 in).
- LC2 side load: 1330 N (300 lbf) horizontal at shoulder height of the main hoop; deflection <= 38 mm (1.5 in).
- LC3 harness load: 1334 N (300 lbf) rearward on the shoulder-harness tab.
- LC4 combined top + side: 2670 N top + 1330 N side simultaneous; each deflection limit still applies.

prompt.title: Human-Powered Vehicle Roll Protection System (tandem)
prompt.brief: Chromoly tubular RPS sized to 2670 N top @ 12 deg (<=51 mm), 1330 N side (<=38 mm), 1334 N harness load cases.

boundary_conditions.mounting: frame fixed at four chassis-anchor points (under seat pan)
boundary_conditions.loads: point loads at hoop top / shoulder / harness tab

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: HPV is low-speed, RPS is quasi-static.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Closed tubular frame with redundant load paths.

eval_coverage: dual stress + deflection pass/fail per case
